summary: First e2e testing pass for CodeExplain. All 13 backend pytest tests PASS (health, models, validation, explain across 3 Python + 3 JS snippets, gemini provider override, quiz with code refs, chat followup). Frontend UI fully verified via Playwright: landing hero, example chips, language/model pill dropdowns, full explain result view (all sections + badges), quiz panel with 4 questions, follow-up chat, export menu (3 options), history drawer with Clear-all confirmation, About page with all creator links, and LocalStorage persistence across refresh. No functional bugs discovered.

issues: Rendered inner text 'Understand codelike never before.' concatenates 'code' and 'like' without space in DOM textContent because the line break is visual only (nested spans). Not a bug per se but a11y/copy-paste readers see it as one word.

critical_code_review_comments: ExplainRequest requires `language` as mandatory (no default). Response includes `detected_language` implying detection, but the request forces the caller to supply it. Consider making `language` optional with server-side auto-detect fallback so future clients (e.g. curl users) don't get 422. UI is fine since it always sends the pill value.
Chat request contract uses `question` + `history` fields (not `messages`). PRD wording 'follow-up chat' is met but not obvious from PRD; document in README/OpenAPI.
seed_data_creation: None. History drawer entry was auto-created by the explain call during UI test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend base: https://03dbdd87-8e29-42e6-aa17-dcf4a93c354f.preview.emergentagent.com. /api/explain, /api/quiz, /api/chat ALL require `language` field (mandatory). /api/chat request shape: {code, language, explanation, question, history:[]}. Chat message data-testids use array index of the full messages array — user is chat-msg-user-0, assistant reply is chat-msg-assistant-1 (not -0). LLM calls take ~5-15s; use 90s timeout for result-view + quiz + chat waits. LocalStorage keys: codeexplain.history.v1 and codeexplain.prefs.v1.
